summary: A small rectangular single-story building with a stepped dark roof and a stonebrick rim at the top of the walls. Walls are primarily horizontal wood planks with vertical log corner posts, rows of glass windows on the sides, and a low stonebrick foundation/paved border surrounding the structure. The roof uses dark nether-brick blocks in a layered stepped pattern with occasional stone slab accents at the corners/steps.

shape.overall_form: rectangular box with a layered/stepped roof and raised stone rim
shape.symmetry: bilaterally symmetrical along the long axis (mirrored front/back)

elements: continuous row windows along the long sides (glass panes)
vertical log corner posts framing the walls
stonebrick slab/ledge wrapping the top of the wall and forming a low border around the base
stepped layered nether_brick roof with small stone slab blocks placed on step corners
no visible doors from the provided views (uncertain location)
rebuild_hints: Start with a rectangular wooden box ~11x19 blocks footprint on a 1-block high stonebrick foundation border inset by one block from the outer paved edge.
Place vertical logs at all four corners front-to-back, fill walls with horizontal wooden planks and a continuous 1-block-high row of glass panes near the top of the walls on the long sides.
Add a stonebrick top rim one block high around the top edge of the walls to serve as a roof base.

Build a nether_brick layered roof: several layers stepping inward on the long sides (sloped by 1 block each layer), with small stone slab blocks placed as corner/step accents and a small flat cap at the very top.
Surround the building with a one-block-wide paved/stonebrick walkway at ground level matching the visible outer border.
uncertainties: Exact interior layout and presence/location of an entrance are not visible in the images.
Precise block counts for width/depth/height are estimated from perspective; actual footprint may differ by a few blocks.
Material variants: wood plank species and specific stone slab/block variants are inferred by color and may be different in source.
